tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Cricketer Reunites with CPR 'Angel' Who Saved His Life
stories.summary: Muhammad Akhtar Hussain reunites with Laura Anderson, the woman who performed 29 minutes of CPR to save his life after he collapsed playing cricket in Hertfordshire.

stories.headline: Four Children with Terminal Brain Cancer Saved by New Cell Therapy
stories.summary: Three children with incurable brain cancers show no evidence of disease years after receiving experimental TAA T-cell therapy, with one DIPG patient alive more than two years later.

stories.headline: Teenage Volleyball Team Wins National Championship in Honor of Fallen Father
stories.summary: Peak Volleyball's 13U team won a national championship and dedicated their victory to Marty Proper, the father of player Tess who suffered a heart attack shortly after the team's qualifier win.

stories.headline: Stolen Guitar Replaced by Compassionate Community After Social Media Appeal
stories.summary: A compassionate social media post by Stefan Roberts about Lucas, an unsheltered man whose guitar was stolen, sparked strangers to gift him a replacement within hours.

stories.headline: Born Without Hands, Indian Freestyler Becomes Football Star Inspired by Ronaldo
stories.summary: Born without hands, an Indian freestyler pursues football and martial arts with passion, turning physical challenge into extraordinary creative excellence.

stories.headline: Teen's Eagle Scout Project Blooms Into Community Blessing Box
stories.summary: One teenager's initiative to help neighbors evolved into a thriving community effort where people continuously stock shelves with food and care.

stories.headline: Thailand Celebrates Historic Bronze at Special Olympics Unified Football World Cup
stories.summary: Thailand's women's unified football team made history winning bronze at the Special Olympics World Cup in Paris, defeating Slovakia in a penalty shootout after a 4-4 draw.

stories.headline: Once-Extinct Numbats Make a Stunning Comeback in Australia
stories.summary: Thanks to decades of zoo breeding and conservation effort, numbats have rebounded from near-extinction to 2,000–3,000 animals—a triumph for persistence.

stories.headline: Indian Cricketer Becomes First Woman to Score Test Century at Lord's
stories.summary: Indian wicketkeeper Yastika Bhatia scored 113 runs to become the first woman cricketer to hit a Test century at Lord's, marking an incredible comeback from a career-threatening knee injury.

stories.headline: Community Rallies to Rebuild After Wildfire Destroys Home
stories.summary: After losing everything to fire, Ashley Okrainec finds her tight-knit community stepping forward with GoFundMe support and hands-on help to rebuild.

stories.headline: Young Volunteers Transform Kentucky Town, Repairing 52 Homes
stories.summary: Teenagers from 13 states spent a week repairing homes and uplifting neighbors across Winchester, Kentucky in an inspiring work camp.

stories.headline: Endangered Loggerhead Turtles Released Into Mediterranean with Hope
stories.summary: Two loggerhead turtles named Flora and Fauna are released into the Mediterranean with satellite trackers, offering new hope for species recovery.

stories.headline: Borneo Forest Recovery Speeds Up When Scientists Cut Back Vines
stories.summary: Cutting vining plants called lianas in logged Borneo forests triples canopy regeneration and carbon capture compared to tree planting alone.

stories.headline: Engineers Create a Battery-Like Device That Captures CO₂ from Air
stories.summary: Engineers Kyle Smith, Paul Rozzi, and JeongA Lee developed a new electrochemical device that pulls carbon dioxide from the atmosphere using saltwater and electricity.
